title: Rooftop solar systems to benefit 4,200 families in Puthalapattu, says Energy Minister
content: Gottipati Ravi Kumar inaugurates 2 kW solar rooftop systems installed free of charge for SC/ST households under PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana at Anakanambattu village

title: ‘Sadbhav Yatra’ has posed political challenge to BJP’s divisive politics, says Congress leader Brijendra Singh
content: Setting out on the padyatra in October last year after he lost the election to Uchana Kalan Assembly constituency by mere 32 votes, Mr. Singh said the yatra had helped him evolve politically

title: Tamil Nadu elections 2026 LIVE: More than 82% voter turnout in State by 5 p.m.
content: Limited bus services affect polling day travel; robots dressed in 'dhavanis' (half-sarees) welcome and assist voters in several booths across the State; several young voters show up in colour-coded outfits, purportedly in support of TVK

title: West Bengal Elections 2026 LIVE: First phase of polling ends as State registers nearly 90% voter turnout till 5 p.m.
content: Sporadic incidents of violence were reported over EVM glitches, voter intimidation and attacks on candidates, including on two from BJP

title: State funding for pension expenditure of universities hiked to 70%, says Minister
content: M.C. Sudhakar has said that this is to address the issue of rising pension liabilities on State universities

title: No government has shown so much solidarity with 'murderous Israeli regime' as Modi government: Congress
content: Congress general secretary in-charge communications Jairam Ramesh also alleged that the Modi government has betrayed all human values, apart from traditional Indian principles and positions

title: The Hindu EducationPlus Career Counselling 2026 to commence across Karnataka from April 25
content: Designed as a one-stop platform for academic and career counselling, the event brings together renowned educationists, career experts, and top institutions to provide personalised guidance.

title: Eight Forest department officials in Kerala trapped inside deep forest, rescued
content: The officials, including two women, were stranded for nearly 16 hours deep within the Nagarampara forest under the Munnar forest division in Idukki following a routine patrolling mission

title: Musalimadugu set for eco-tourism boost with ₹20 lakh funding
content: Participating in a review meet on Haritha Andhra Pradesh Mission, Collector assures focus will be on improving roads, essential facilities, and creating visitor-friendly arrangements that do not disturb the natural setting

title: Nithin Raj death: Order on anticipatory bail pleas of accused on April 25
content: Both accused have been booked under the Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act

title: Coal 'scam' case: Court remands I-PAC director Vinesh Chandel to 14 days in judicial custody
content: Chandel has moved the Patiala House Court seeking regular bail; the judge issued a notice on the plea and asked the ED to file its reply

title: Belagavi doctors save girl from rare disorder
content: Initial tests revealed that she was suffering from a rare and life-threatening condition — splenic torsion

title: Bengaluru firm Dairy Day ropes in Salesforce to power nextgen growth
content: Bengaluru-based ice cream, candy and cone manufacturer aims fast growth, increased market footprint and huge revenue growth

title: Tamil Nadu election: Sri Lankan refugee camp resident casts his first vote as Indian citizen
content: “Most first-time voters are in their early 20s. I am almost 40. But I feel as if my life as an Indian has just begun, and my the future of my children as Indian citizens is assured,” R. Gokuleswaran said

title: U.P. govt. apprehends unrest on May 1, Yogi Adityanath tells officials to remain alert
content: Industrial unrest will not be tolerated under any circumstances, and such malicious attempts must be firmly thwarted, a government statement said

title: Nandyal MP prioritises safe drinking water for schools, underprivileged areas
content: Inaugurating a mineral water plant at Talamudipi village in Nandikotkur constituency, Byreddy Shabari urges villagers to take responsibility for the maintenance of thel water plants built with MP funds

title: Sixteen workers injured in major fire at chemical factory in Gujarat's Bharuch
content: All the injured workers were shifted to nearby hospitals for treatment, and their condition is reported to be stable

title: Tarluvada, a village of 3,000 near Vizag, prepares for Google AI data centre foundation on April 28
content: Nestled in Anandapuram mandal outside Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation limits, the hamlet will host Google’s first hyperscale 1 GW AI data centre outside the United States; the foundation stone is to be laid on April 28.

title: Guntur Municipal Corporation fast-tracks desilting of 180 km drains
content: Civic body sets May 31 deadline for ₹3.68 crore works to curb flooding; contractors to ensure 90-day maintenance

title: Delhi court sends man accused of killing IRS officer’s daughter to 4-day police custody
content: With his face covered by a cloth, the accused was produced in the jam-packed courtroom where the magistrate asked Rahul Meena about the injuries shown in the medico-legal report to which the accused said he got hurt while getting down from the terrace

title: Karnataka SSLC exam-1 results: Chikkamagaluru topper followed a goal-oriented study plan for 7 hours everyday
content: Brunda M. Tapse wishes to study science at PUC and pursue her higher studies in the engineering stream.

title: No one will be able to infiltrate Bengal after BJP wins polls: Amit Shah
content: Amit Shah also flagged the alleged decline of Balagarh's traditional boat-making industry, claiming that it had suffered under the present dispensation

title: Bengal polls: BJP MLA Agnimitra Paul's car attacked in Asansol, rear windowpanes shattered
content: Stones were thrown at my moving car as I was exiting a polling booth premises. The rear window was shattered. It is very clear who is behind this. This is an attempt to influence the election, says BJP candidate Agnimitra Paul

title: Karnataka SSLC exam-1: Focused preparatory strategy helps Dakshina Kannada and Udupi achieve record performance
content: 98.4% pass percentage recorded this year is highest recorded by Dakshina Kannada during the last 12 years

title: Policeman stabbed at polling booth in Poompuhar constituency; ex-serviceman arrested
content: Preliminary inquiry revealed that the suspect allegedly harboured hostility towards police personnel and had been involved in a similar assault on a policeman a few years ago

title: West Bengal voter turnout makes me certain of BJP’s landslide victory: PM Modi
content: “I had 'jhalmuri' in Bengal, but it seems that the TMC leaders are feeling the heat of the chillies,” PM Modi said

title: Karnataka SSLC exam-1 results: State records ‘historic’ 94.1% pass percentage
content: The results have shown a marked improvement, with a 14.06 percentage point increase compared to the 80.04% recorded last year

title: Karnataka SSLC exam-1 results: State toppers reflect on their journey to 625/625
content: Karnataka Class 10 toppers tell The Hindu how they achieved the perfect score and the intense preparation that helped them shine

title: Karnataka SSLC exam-1 results: A top-scoring student from Raichur continues his success streak in Class 10
content: Sukhadeva told The Hindu that he only studied four to five hours a day. Though he initially took tuitions for a month, he later began studying at home by himself.

title: Karnataka SSLC exam-1 results: Some play, some study is the secret to Begaluru topper’s perfect score
content: Initially, Dhanush studied only a little, choosing to play sports like chess, cricket, and tennis alongside. He took a more disciplines approach as the exams neared.

title: India’s butterfly boom: How Nature walks, parks, and trails are sparking a new conservation movement
content: From Bengaluru’s urban forests to the Northeast’s biodiversity hotspots, enthusiasts and experts are turning butterfly-watching into a powerful gateway to ecology, education, and sustainable travel

title: India-Africa ties signal “stability in a turbulent world”: Jaishankar
content: At IAFS-IV launch, EAM highlights India-Africa partnership, development cooperation, and strategic engagement

title: Delhi woman rape and murder case: Rajasthan police probing rape charge against accused man
content: The culprit is a serial offender, and has been accussed of sexually assaulting a woman in Rajasthan’s Alwar haours before committing the crime in Delhi

title: In Photos: Tamil Nadu votes
content: The election in Tamil Nadu started with long queues of people waiting outside the polling stations to exercise their franchise

title: Fight in PSL: Faheem points bat at Amir after wicket, tension erupts - Watch
content: The moment came on the third ball of the 17th over of the first innings when Amir bowled a slow bouncer to Faheem Ashraf. Faheem tried to play an upper-cut but edged it to wicketkeeper Mohammad Rizwan.

title: Founder of one of Wall Street’s biggest law firms Sullivan & Cromwell sends apology letter to judge after AI 'messes up' court filing; says: We sincerely ...
content: Sullivan & Cromwell has apologized to a Federal judge for AI-driven errors in a bankruptcy case filing. The motion contained fictitious case names, fabricated quotes, and non-existent statutes, attributed to "AI hallucinations." The firm stated it failed to follow its verification protocols, regretting the burden on the court and parties involved.

title: 'Will you just khi khi?' Oppn calls PM 'weak' over silence on Trump's 'hellhole' post
content: A political storm has erupted after US President Donald Trump reshared a post labeling India a "hell hole." Opposition parties, including the Congress and Mahua Moitra, have sharply criticized the remarks and Prime Minister Narendra Modi's perceived silence, questioning his leadership. The controversy stems from a podcast by Michael Savage, which Trump amplified on his platform.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 m
content: Michael and Susan Dell have pledged $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This significant donation will focus on integrating AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education. The project, expected to open by 2030, aims to enhance Austin's healthcare capabilities and advance medical innovation.

title: 'If didi had given money ...': Man who raped, killed IRS officer's daughter shows no remorse
content: A former domestic help, Rahul Meena, allegedly used a spare key to enter a south Delhi apartment and rape and murder his former employer's daughter. He is suspected of strangling her, attempting to access a locker, and fleeing after stealing cash and jewelry. Meena, described as remorseful, is also being investigated for a similar crime in Rajasthan.

title: Bhooth Bangla BO: Akshay Kumar starrer marches towards Rs 100 crore in week 1
content: 'Bhooth Bangla', directed by Priyadarshan and starring Akshay Kumar, Tabu, Paresh Rawal, Wamiqa Gabbi, Rajpal Yadav, Mithila Palkar and Asrani, is closing in on the Rs. 100 crore India net milestone as it completes its first week in theatres. With a cumulative India net of Rs. 79.02 crore and a worldwide gross of Rs. 127.37 crore after seven days, the U/A certified horror-comedy has delivered a strong opening week despite competing against 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' in its fifth week and a full IPL 2026 season. Marking Akshay Kumar and Priyadarshan's reunion after 16 years, the 145-minute supernatural thriller follows Arjun Acharya confronting the demonic entity Vadhusur in his ancestral home in Mangalpur, featuring a nostalgic ensemble of Paresh Rawal, Rajpal Yadav and the late Asrani.

title: 'We are installing tracking software in your machines': Meta to US employees
content: Meta is installing new tracking software on US employees' computers to capture mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes for AI training. This initiative aims to improve AI agents' ability to perform work tasks autonomously, like using shortcuts. Meta assures employees the data won't be used for performance reviews, only for enhancing AI models.

title: Watch: Stones thrown at Bengal BJP Agnimitra Paul's car, rear window shattered
content: BJP leader Agnimitra Paul's car was vandalized in a stone-pelting incident in Asansol after she exited a polling booth. Paul alleged a deliberate attempt to influence the elections and filed a complaint. Contesting from Asansol South, she expressed confidence in the BJP's prospects, calling the election a 'war to save Bengal' focused on jobs and development.

title: Mexico safety concerns rise after deadly pyramid attack, president responds
content: A deadly shooting at Teotihuacan has intensified scrutiny of Mexico’s security readiness ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The planned attack, which killed a tourist and injured several others, exposed gaps at a major landmark. While authorities call it isolated, the incident underscores broader concerns about safeguarding visitors during a globally significant tournament approaching quickly.

title: Mike Vrabel–Dianna Russini drama worsens amid new affair claims
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and the popular NFL insider who used to work at The New York Times’ Dianna Russini, continue to be in the eye of the storm as their alleged affair rumors swirl as their spouses remain distant. Though both of them have denied engaging in an affair and betraying their respective spouses, it seems the story has taken a turn for the worse.

title: Son takes auto driver father, homemaker mother on first flight- video
content: A viral reel captured a son's joy as he took his parents on their first flight, a moment born from years of his father's sacrifice as an auto driver.  This simple trip, funded by his success in professional jump rope, marked a profound shift, finally allowing his parents to experience life beyond necessity and hear his father's proud words.

title: Aluminium foil on door handles: Trending US hack but is it smart?
content: A viral 2026 home hack involves wrapping door handles in aluminium foil across the US homes, claiming to reduce static shocks and deter intruders. While foil's conductivity can disperse electrical charges, its security benefits are primarily psychological, offering minimal actual protection. Experts advise it is a supplementary trick, not a substitute for robust security measures.

title: 3 players who could replace Ayush Mhatre at no.3 for CSK against MI
content: Chennai Super Kings face a significant blow as top-order batsman Ayush Mhatre is out with a hamstring tear. His absence at number three creates a void ahead of their clash with Mumbai Indians. The team is now considering options like Urvil Patel, Sarfaraz Khan, or Kartik Sharma to fill the crucial batting position.

title: Dhoni plays with 'Champak', return buzz grows ahead of CSK vs MI clash
content: MS Dhoni, recovering from a calf strain, was seen practicing and playfully interacting with a robot dog at the Wankhede Stadium. His return to the field for Chennai Super Kings' clash against Mumbai Indians is highly anticipated, with former cricketer Ravichandran Ashwin confident in his fitness and ability to bolster CSK's finishing power.

title: 'Let the notice come, we'll reply': Kharge reacts to EC ultimatum over 'terrorist' remark
content: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge has responded to an Election Commission notice regarding his "terrorist" remarks on Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Kharge stated his party would reply to the EC's ultimatum, which warned of action if he failed to explain his stand within 24 hours. The controversial comment was made during campaigning in Chennai.

title: Voodoo outrage: Akola self-styled 'godman' lifts kids with his teeth in unsafe rituals
content: Disturbing videos show a self-styled godman in Akola district subjecting children to unsafe, superstition-driven rituals. Chetan Sunil Mule, known as 'Gulal Shesh Maharaj', has been holding such gatherings for years, claiming to cure illnesses. Authorities have summoned all self-styled godmen in the area following widespread outrage and demands for action.

title: After killing IRS officer's daughter, accused stole Rs 2.5 lakh: New details in Delhi murder
content: A 22-year-old woman, daughter of an IRS officer, was allegedly raped and murdered in her south Delhi home. Police arrested a former domestic worker, Rahul Meena, who is also suspected of theft and a similar crime in Rajasthan. Meena was dismissed from his job due to repeated complaints about borrowing money.

title: A future without cigarettes: Britain’s long game against smoking
content: The UK Parliament has passed a landmark bill to create the first smoke-free generation by prohibiting cigarette sales to anyone born after December 31, 2008. This legislation, awaiting royal assent, also grants the government enhanced powers to regulate tobacco and vaping products, aiming to significantly reduce smoking-related deaths and protect future generations from addiction.

title: Man assaults woman outside liquor shop in Gurgaon; viral video sparks outrage
content: A heated argument outside a Gurugram liquor shop escalated into a physical altercation. A viral video shows a man slapping a woman multiple times after she allegedly hurled abuses. The woman retaliated, slapping him and pulling his T-shirt. Bystanders attempted to intervene. Police have taken note of the video but no formal complaint has been filed yet.

title: 2 rapes, 1 murder: Crime trail began in Alwar, ended in Delhi with IRS officer’s daughter’s killing
content: A 23-year-old man, accused of raping and killing an IIT graduate in Delhi, allegedly committed another violent sexual assault in Rajasthan hours prior. He arrived in Delhi by ambulance, then targeted his former employer's home, gaining entry through familiar security measures. Police are investigating both incidents jointly.

title: Why did Trump 'fire' US navy secretary abruptly amid Hormuz blockade? What reports claim
content: Navy Secretary John Phelan has been dismissed following reported tensions with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Deputy Defense Secretary Steve Feinberg. Disagreements over the pace of naval modernization and shipbuilding priorities, as well as Phelan's direct communication with President Trump, led to his removal. Hung Cao has been appointed acting Navy secretary.

title: India a ‘tough nut to crack’: US Trade Representative makes big statement as 3-day trade deal talks conclude
content: Under the proposed interim trade deal, the US has lowered tariffs on India from 50% to 18%. In August last year, Trump had imposed a 25% reciprocal tariff on India and an additional 25% penal tariff for India’s crude oil purchases from Russia.

title: Mitchell Starc set to join Delhi Capitals; selection headache on the cards
content: Mitchell Starc is set to join Delhi Capitals for IPL 2026 on Friday after Cricket Australia clearances, targeting a May 1 return against Rajasthan Royals. The injured fast bowler's arrival presents a selection dilemma for the Axar Patel-led side, which needs strong performances in upcoming matches to boost playoff hopes. Starc's gradual return will be managed carefully.

title: 'Shut up Trump': Iran mocks US president with AI-video over ceasefire extension on 'Pakistan's request'
content: Iran's consulate in Hyderabad shared an AI-generated video mocking US President Donald Trump's announcement of a ceasefire extension with Iran. The clip humorously depicts a fictional Trump agreeing to the extension at Pakistan's request, following Iran's accusations of US "disregard and lack of good faith" amid the ongoing peace negotiations.

title: Zelenskyy calls on 'big players' Trump, PM Modi and Xi to put pressure on Russia
content: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy urged global leaders, including PM Modi, to pressure Putin to end the war, stating mediation alone is insufficient. He emphasized unified international action over compromise.  Meanwhile, Ukraine is bolstering defence ties in the Middle East, signing drone cooperation agreements with Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and the UAE.

title: US intercepts 3 Iranian oil tankers near India, Malaysia amid maritime blockade
content: US forces have intercepted at least three Iranian oil tankers in Asian waters, redirecting them away from key shipping lanes near India, Malaysia, and Sri Lanka. This action intensifies Washington's maritime blockade on Iran, disrupting global energy flows. The US Central Command has directed numerous vessels to turn back, highlighting the growing impact on international trade amidst stalled diplomatic efforts.

title: Women entry in Sabarimala: SC says it won’t rely on ‘WhatsApp University’
content: The Supreme Court, while hearing a case on gender discrimination in temples, cautioned against relying on information from 'WhatsApp University.' A nine-judge bench, led by the Chief Justice, emphasized that while eminent authors' views are respected, personal opinions are not sufficient for judicial decisions. The court acknowledged the difficulty in defining essential religious practices.

title: 'Thunderous jolt': PM Modi hits back at TMC’s 'jhalmuri' dig with 'chillies' barb
content: Prime Minister Modi playfully responded to opposition criticism about his unscheduled jhalmuri stop in West Bengal, stating the state will celebrate BJP's victory with sweets and the popular snack.  He suggested the TMC felt the "heat of chillies" from his brief break, while the TMC accused him of staging a "scripted drama" and humiliating an Adivasi leader.

title: India’s crude oil production falls for 11th consecutive year; gas also sees a dip
content: According to data from the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, crude production fell 2.5 per cent to 28 million metric tonnes in 2025–26. This marks a cumulative drop of 22 per cent since 2014–15.

title: Punjab Police, Congress Leader Push For Ban On 'Lawrence Of Punjab' Web Series
content: Punjab Police has also written to the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting, seeking to block public access to the series.

title: 8 Killed, 2 Injured As Vehicle Falls Into Gorge In Uttarakhand's Chamba
content: The victims, residents of Thyoldhar village in the Ghansali, were returning home after performing the last rites of a villager in Haridwar.

title: Amid US Blockade, Iran Says It Has Banked First Proceeds From Hormuz Tolls
content: While strikes around the region have mostly ceased since the two-week-old truce began, there has been no letup in the stand-off over the crucial trade route, with both sides seeking economic leverage.

title: Man Who Killed Wife In Delhi In 1986 Arrested, Ending 4-Decade Hunt
content: The case remained unsolved for nearly 40 years due to a lack of leads and the absence of modern investigative tools at the time.

title: Pentagon Denies Reports Claiming Hormuz Mine Clearance Will Take 6 Months
content: Iran has vowed not to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as long as the United States blockades its ports, with the blocked waterway sharply driving up oil and gas prices and disrupting the global economy.

title: Wife Refuses To Return Home, Man Sets In-Laws' House On Fire In Maharashtra
content: The woman had been staying at her maternal home for a while following a dispute with her husband Mithun Padvi.

title: US-Iran War Can Push Over 30 Million People Into Poverty, UN Official Warns
content: Fertiliser shortages - worsened by the Strait of Hormuz closure - have already lowered agricultural productivity, UNDP chief Alexander De Croo said.

title: 'Delays, Hurdles': BJP Chief Nitin Nabin Blames Bengal Government For SIR Deletions
content: In an exclusive interview with NDTV Editor-in-Chief and CEO Rahul Kanwal, Nabin said the Election Commission ultimately works in line with the recommendations of the administrative officials, who are representatives of the state government.

title: Top Court's Bengal "President's Rule" Question To Probe Agency In I-PAC Case
content: The Supreme Court said an argument of breakdown of constitutional machinery will have "far-reaching consequences", as it can lead to President's rule.

title: 'If There's Malpractice, Action Will Follow': BJP Chief Nitin Nabin On I-PAC Raids
content: BJP chief Nitin Nabin hinted that political consultancy firm I-PAC was doing more than political work for the Trinamool.

title: UP Man Kills Wife After Fight Over Going To Gym, Sits With Body For 7 Hours
content: After the accused killed his wife, he placed her body on the bed and remained in the house for almost 7 hours.

title: Climate Change Kept Off Agenda For G7 Meet In Paris To 'Avoid Row With US'
content: The office of France's ecology minister Monique Barbut had said the Group of Seven would focus on "less contentious issues" to appease the US.

title: West Bengal, Tamil Nadu Elections 2026 LIVE Updates: Voter Turnout Crosses 80%
content: West Bengal, Tamil Nadu Election Voting 2026 LIVE Updates: Around 3.6 crore voters in 152 of Bengal's 294 constituencies across 16 districts are expected to vote in the first phase today.

title: Fast Boats, Masks, Rifles: How Iran Guards Seized India-Bound Ship In Hormuz
content: The video shows speedboats with Iranian flags approaching the vessels, after which soldiers wearing face masks climb the ships, donning rifles.

title: Delhi Court Asks Google, X To Remove Videos Of Arvind Kejriwal's Case Hearing
content: The court noted submissions by Meta that it had already taken down some URLs flagged by the High Court's Registrar General.

title: 'Trinamool Must Be Anxious, We Aren't': BJP Chief To NDTV On SIR
content: The BJP has no concerns about the special intensive revision (SIR) exercise in West Bengal, the party's national chief Nitin Nabin said today

title: "I Ate Jhalmuri, But Trinamool Felt The Sting": PM Modi In Bengal
content: Jhalmuri, a popular street food in Bengal, hit the headlines when PM Modi, during a poll campaign in Jhargram last week, stopped to enjoy the snack.

title: CCB books three in ₹1.8-crore job scam, 55 aspirants cheated
content:
The Central Crime Branch officials on Tuesday registered an FIR against three men allegedly involved in a major job fraud, cheating over 55 job aspirants on the pretext of providing employment and making away with ₹1.8 crore.

Based on the complaint filed by one of the victims, identified as N. Surendranath Rao Mirajkar, the police charged V.K. Ramesh, D. Raghavendra, and B.S. Devikumar, charging them for cheating and fraud. The accused, according to the complaint, allegedly collected large sums of money from candidates between 2019 and 2023, with the promise of helping them obtain government jobs.
